sitrep: F-15E SHOT DOWN: US F-15E Strike Eagle shot down over southern Iran near Qeshm Island (Strait of Hormuz). 1 crew member rescued by US special forces. 2nd crew member MISSING — Iran hunting with civilian bounty. Israel cancelled planned strikes to avoid hampering rescue. If 2nd pilot captured = first US POW since war began. 5th US aircraft lost.

OIL SURGES $112: Brent $112.42 (+$6.89/+6.5%). WTI $111.54 — SURPASSED Brent for first time (Rigzone). F-15 downing + Gulf refinery fires = panic buying. War premium fully entrenched. Highest sustained levels since war began.

KUWAIT MINA AL-AHMADI HIT: Iran drones struck Kuwait's largest refinery (466K bbl/day) — fires across several operational units. Contained, no injuries. Emergency protocols activated. SECOND time this refinery targeted.
UAE HABSHAN + EGA: Habshan gas facility fire from intercepted debris. Emirates Global Aluminium site (Abu Dhabi) forced shutdown from missile/drone strike — significant industrial damage.
B1 BRIDGE DESTROYED: Century-old Tehran-Karaj bridge destroyed overnight by US/Israeli airstrike. 8+ killed, 95 wounded (D34 strikes). Major infrastructure — connects capital to 2nd largest metro.

PASTEUR INSTITUTE + RED CRESCENT: Strikes hit Pasteur Institute (century-old medical research center, Tehran) and Red Crescent aid warehouse (Bushehr province). International condemnation expected. 600+ schools/education centers hit total.

IDF 70+ STRIKES: IDF carried out 70+ strikes in Iran in past 24 hours — weapons production sites including central Ministry of Defense complex in Tehran. Air defense systems, launch sites, ballistic missile storage. Beirut simultaneously struck.

ATIMI KILLED: IDF confirmed Makram Atimi, Iranian ballistic missile chief, killed in Kermanshah area D34 strike along with several battalion commanders from central ballistic missile unit. Significant decapitation of missile command.

FIRST WESTERN HORMUZ TRANSIT: French CMA CGM container ship Kribi (Malta-flagged) crossed Hormuz April 2 — first Western vessel since war began. 3 Omani tankers also transited hugging Omani coastline. Hormuz cracking open but only ~5-7 vessels/day.

CASUALTIES UPDATE: AJ official tracker: 2,076+ killed Iran, 26,500+ wounded. Lebanon 1,318+ (125+ children). Israel: 19 civilians killed, 6,594+ wounded. US: 15 KIA + 520+ wounded + 1 missing F-15E crew. Novara Media reports military hiding true extent of casualties.

APRIL 6 = 3 DAYS: Power grid deadline NOW 3 days away. Trump: 'assault on infrastructure hasn't even started.' Threatens power + desalination plants. Iran FM rejects US proposal as 'one-sided and unfair.' World leaders opt for diplomacy, not force for Hormuz (Norway FM). No deal imminent.
INTERNET 792+ HRS: 35 days. Longest in history. 90M isolated. 85,000+ structures damaged.

مینا الاحمدی کویت: پهپادهای ایران. آتش‌سوزی. ۴۶۶ هزار بشکه/روز.
حبشان امارات + EGA: آتش‌سوزی گاز + آلومینیوم تعطیل.
پل B1 تخریب: تهران-کرج. ۸+ کشته. ۹۵ زخمی.
پاستور + هلال‌احمر: انستیتو پاستور تهران + انبار هلال‌احمر بوشهر. ۶۰۰+ مدرسه.
IDF ۷۰+ حمله: سایت‌های تسلیحاتی تهران + وزارت دفاع + بیروت.
عتیمی کشته: فرمانده موشک بالستیک ایران در کرمانشاه.

dailyRows.assessment: IRGC 'arsenal intact' claim is the most significant D17 development. If true, Iran has been deliberately rationing — using old stock while preserving modern missiles. This reframes the entire attrition narrative. War may have much longer tail than assumed.

theoryEvaluation.body: 5th US aircraft lost. First fighter jet since D12 KC-135. One pilot rescued, second MISSING. Iran hunting with bounty. If captured = first US POW since war began. Changes political calculus: Trump cannot claim air superiority while losing fighters. But F-15E downing may HARDEN US resolve — retaliatory escalation likely. Israel cancelled strikes to support rescue = coalition coordination under stress.

theoryEvaluation.body: Haaretz/CNN report Iran retains HALF of missile launchers + massive drone fleet. Directly contradicts CENTCOM's repeated '90% degraded' claims. D35 at 50 missiles = still well above decay model. NBC data graphics confirm sustained capability. The 'weeks not months' narrative is not supported by actual Iranian capabilities.

Iran expanding from military to economic targets. Oil $112 = market sees extended supply disruption. WTI surpassing Brent = US-centric demand panic.

theoryEvaluation.body: French CMA CGM Kribi = first Western vessel through Hormuz since D1. Positive signal but at ~5-7 vessels/day vs pre-war 62. Iran allowing selective transit while maintaining leverage. Safe passage framework expanding despite F-15E incident. But refinery fires contradict de-escalation.

Missing pilot prevents any pause. Oil $112 = maximum economic pressure. April 6 power grid strikes remain MOST LIKELY outcome.

publishingDeepDive: PAPER/INK SUPPLY: Iran imports 70%+ of printing paper. Hormuz closure + sanctions = supply chain severed. Domestic paper mills operating at estimated 20-30% capacity due to power disruptions. Quality declining.

PRINTING CAPACITY: Tehran's major printing houses (Offset, Sepehr, etc.) operating intermittently due to power outages. 768+ hour internet blackout prevents digital prepress. Estimated 80-90% capacity reduction.

DISTRIBUTION NETWORKS: Domestic book distribution (Shahre Ketab, Book City, etc.) severely disrupted. 82,000+ structures damaged. Physical bookstores in strike zones closed. Online ordering impossible without internet.

CENSORSHIP APPARATUS: Ministry of Culture and Islamic Guidance (Ershad) presumably still operating but capacity unknown. Book permits may be delayed or suspended. Paradoxically, war may reduce censorship bandwidth.

PUBLISHER SOLVENCY: Small and medium publishers facing existential crisis. No sales, no distribution, no printing capacity. University presses better positioned (institutional backing) but still severely impacted. Expect consolidation/closures post-war.

DIGITAL ALTERNATIVES: Iran's digital book ecosystem (Taaghche, Fidibo) non-functional during internet blackout. Post-restoration, digital may recover faster than print. However, academic institutions require physical copies for cataloging and archival.

hormuzReopeningTimeline_fa.detail: فعلاً ~۵-۱۰ با عوارض یوآنی. نیاز: چارچوب چندجانبه + اسکورت.
مهام ۳/۷ تأیید. نیاز به ناوهای MCM تخصصی.
لویدز کل خلیج. حق‌بیمه ۳.۸٪ (از ۰.۰۲٪). نیاز: مین‌روبی + ۹۰ روز بدون حادثه.
مین + بیمه + بندر + باب‌المندب. ۱,۰۰۰+ نفتکش معطل.

strandedCargo.location: Jebel Ali Free Zone + Hamriyah Free Zone
strandedCargo.detail: Most cargo insured under pre-war marine policies. Claims processing delayed — Lloyd's overwhelmed. Perishables (~15% by value) written off. Non-perishable goods safe in bonded storage but incurring daily storage fees (~$150/container/day).
strandedCargo.detail_fa: اکثر بار بیمه. پردازش خسارت تأخیر. فاسدشدنی (~۱۵٪) از دست رفته. غیرفاسدشدنی در انبار اما هزینه روزانه (~$۱۵۰/کانتینر/روز).

insuranceCostIndex.note: D34: Hengam Island (Hormuz entrance) struck + tanker off Qatar D34 + Aqua 1 D33 + Dubai D32 = 3 maritime incidents in 3 days. Hull 12.5% = effectively uninsurable. Gulf-wide port anchorage unsafe.

cumulativeByCountry._note: Estimated cumul ~3,185 missiles / ~5,062 drones through D34. D34: Trump primetime + Kharazi struck + oil surged. Hengam Island + tanker off Qatar + Tel Aviv 14 wounded.

estimatedStockpiles.note: Based on IISS/CSIS pre-war estimates minus confirmed expenditure (~3,185 fired). Assumes no resupply.
Based on IRGC D16 claim 'most weapons cache intact, using decade-old missiles.' If true, Iran has 4-9 months of sustained fire.
Midpoint. Accounts for some resupply from North Korea/China pipeline + hidden stockpiles minus battle damage.

oilScenarioBands.note: Projections based on Goldman Sachs ($110 avg), Citi ($120+ if sustained), IEA worst-case. Ceasefire scenario assumes phased Hormuz reopening. Bab al-Mandab closure = both chokepoints blocked.
oilScenarioBands.note_fa: پیش‌بینی‌ها بر اساس گلدمن ساکس ($110)، سیتی ($120+)، IEA بدترین حالت. آتش‌بس = بازگشایی مرحله‌ای هرمز. بستن باب‌المندب = هر دو تنگه.

hormuzTransit.note: Pre-war: ~62 vessels/day through Hormuz (21% of global oil). Dropped to 0 within 72 hours. Iran's yuan-based toll system began D15. Pakistan Aframax first non-Iranian transit D26. Currently ~10/day via safe passage framework. Full recovery requires mine clearance (months post-ceasefire).
hormuzTransit.note_fa: قبل جنگ: ~۶۲ کشتی/روز (۲۱٪ نفت جهان). ظرف ۷۲ ساعت صفر شد. عوارض یوآنی D15. پاکستان اولین عبور D26. فعلاً ~۱۰/روز. بازیابی کامل = ماه‌ها مین‌روبی.

ceasefireDeadline.label: April 6, 8PM ET
ceasefireDeadline.context: Trump paused power plant strikes until this deadline. If no deal: power grid destruction = 90M Iranians without electricity.
ceasefireDeadline.context_fa: ترامپ حمله نیروگاه‌ها تا این مهلت متوقف. اگر بدون معامله: شبکه برق = ۹۰ میلیون بدون برق.

branchIfDeal.detail: Likely phased: Hormuz reopening first, strikes pause, humanitarian corridors.
MCM vessels deploy. Safe passage expands to 30+ vessels/day.
Markets price in de-escalation. Gulf exports resume partially.
Insurance normalizes. Mines cleared. Bab al-Mandab safe.

branchIfNoDeal.detail: 90M without electricity. Hospitals, water treatment, communications collapse.
Mass civilian casualties. International condemnation. UN emergency session.
Houthi likely closes Bab al-Mandab. Both chokepoints blocked. Global recession.
Coalition fractures. China/Russia escalate diplomacy. Ground ops on Kharg Island.

events.detail: US + Israel + GCC unified. UAE, Saudi, Qatar hosting US forces. Hormuz blocked by Iran.
Rejected war powers resolution. Gave Trump political cover.
First non-US allied death (CWO Frion). France: 'never' Hormuz during war.
Resigned: 'Iran not a threat.' First senior US official to break.
Saudi: 'right to military action' — warning to US. 12 FM joint statement.
Expelled US military attache. 22-country Hormuz coalition forming WITHOUT US.
Expelled Iranian ambassador. Proxy network fracturing.
All declined to join Hormuz coalition. US increasingly isolated.
Joint condemnation of strikes. Ghalibaf threatened attacks if islands occupied.
Called for 'immediate cessation' — multilateral language but no enforcement.
5-point peace plan. Great-power alternative framework bypassing US.
Nikkei -2.1%, Kospi -3.9%. Economic allies signaling war is unsustainable.

decapitationTracker.name: Ayatollah Khamenei
decapitationTracker.replacement: Mojtaba Khamenei (son) designated by Assembly of Experts D9. Absent since D8 — status unknown.
decapitationTracker.significance: Highest-value target. Decapitation triggered succession crisis. Mojtaba's absence raises questions.

decapitationTracker.name: Ali Larijani
decapitationTracker.replacement: No direct replacement. Diplomatic channel lost.
decapitationTracker.significance: Key back-channel figure. Death narrowed diplomatic options.

decapitationTracker.name: Soleimani (nephew)
decapitationTracker.significance: Symbolic — Soleimani name carries weight in IRGC.

decapitationTracker.name: Adm. Tangsiri
decapitationTracker.replacement: No confirmed successor. Safe passage expanding despite death = institutional not personal.
decapitationTracker.significance: Most significant naval decapitation. Designed the Hormuz blockade strategy.

decapitationTracker.name: ~40 senior officials
decapitationTracker.replacement: IRGC promoting from within. Command continuity maintained at institutional level.
decapitationTracker.significance: Cumulative loss. CENTCOM: 2/3 arms manufacturing destroyed.

decapitationTracker.name: Kamal Kharazi
decapitationTracker.replacement: Wife killed. Back-channel to Pakistan disrupted. Diplomatic momentum damaged.
decapitationTracker.significance: Was overseeing the China-Pakistan peace plan channel. His wounding may derail the most promising diplomatic track.

decapitationTracker.name: Mojtaba Khamenei
decapitationTracker.replacement: N/A — he IS the replacement. Zolghadr named SNSC secretary D24 = institutional continuity without visible leader.
decapitationTracker.significance: 26 days absent. Written statement only (D13). No video. Speculation: injured, hiding, or dead. Regime functioning without visible head of state.
